project.name: PC Interior Portfolio
project.description: A highly realistic interactive portfolio where the user explores a PC case interior. Each component represents a section (CPU=Home, GPU=Skills, RAM=About, etc.).

desktop.description: Full-screen background image of PC interior. Invisible interactive zones (hotspots) overlaid on components.
desktop.navigation: Top fixed 'System Monitor' bar that expands to show content.

mobile.description: Since the wide PC view doesn't fit portrait, use a 'System Specs' list where each item is a card styled like a component (e.g., 'GPU: Skills' card with a fan icon).
mobile.navigation: Sticky top bar with hamburger menu or horizontal scroll tabs.

BackgroundCanvas.description: The main container holding the PC image.
BackgroundCanvas.alt: High-end watercooled PC interior with RGB lighting
BackgroundCanvas.styles: fixed inset-0 w-full h-full object-cover z-0

Hotspot.interaction: Hover: Glow effect + Tooltip. Click: Open TopBar content.

TopBar.description: The 'HUD' or 'BIOS' bar. Always visible at top.
TopBar.content: Shows 'System Status' (Time, CPU Temp mock, etc.) and the Active Section Title.
TopBar.styles: fixed top-0 left-0 w-full h-16 z-50 glassmorphism flex items-center justify-between px-6 border-b border-white/10

ContentPanel.description: The area that displays the actual portfolio content.
ContentPanel.behavior: Slides down from the TopBar or overlays the screen with a glass effect when a component is clicked.
ContentPanel.styles: fixed top-16 left-0 w-full h-[calc(100vh-4rem)] z-40 bg-black/90 backdrop-blur-md overflow-y-auto p-8 transform transition-transform duration-500

assets.icons: Lucide React (Cpu, HardDrive, Zap, Monitor, Layers, User, Mail, FileText)

instructions_to_main_agent: 2. Create a 'Layout' component that houses the BackgroundCanvas and TopBar.
3. Use the 'pc_interior_bg' as the absolute background.

4. Implement 'Hotspot' components using absolute positioning percentages (e.g., top: 40%, left: 50%) to map over the image components. You will need to trial-and-error the positions or create a debug mode to drag them.
5. The 'ContentPanel' should be hidden by default and animate in when a hotspot is clicked.
6. Use 'Rajdhani' font for all headers to give that sci-fi/tech look.
7. Ensure the mobile view falls back to a list of styled cards because the interactive map won't work well on small vertical screens.
universal_guidelines: Do not use gradients for dark colors.
Ensure text contrast is high (White on Black/Dark Grey).
